purpose: Manifest-driven registry and runtime for "loopholes" — declared host↔jail permeability points (audio socket pass-through, Claude OAuth broker TLS-intercept, host-process view). src/loopholes.py loads/validates JSONC manifests from three sources (bundled wheel dir < user dir < workspace yolo-jail.jsonc overrides) and translates them into container runtime flags (-v, --device, --add-host, -e). src/cli/loopholes_runtime.py owns process lifecycle: per-jail host-side daemons (builtin cgroup-delegate + journal bridge + external services), the host-wide broker singleton, and per-jail broker relay supervision. src/cli/loopholes_cmd.py is the `yolo loopholes` Typer subcommand group.

files.role: Registry: manifest schema v1 parse/validate (pyjson5), 3-source discovery+merge, requires-gating (active/inactive), runtime_args_for() → container flags, manifest_host_daemon_specs(), run_doctor_checks(), set_enabled() manifest rewrite

Runtime: LoopholeDaemon handles; start_loopholes/stop_loopholes; builtin cgroup-delegate daemon (JSON-line socket protocol); builtin journal bridge (framed binary protocol); external service spawner; broker singleton (_broker_* PID/lock/socket/ping); per-jail broker relay supervision (_relay_*); GPU/ROCm/nix host probes
Bundled loophole: transport=none; Pulse+PipeWire socket bind mounts (rw), asound.conf mount, /dev/snd --device, PULSE_SERVER/PIPEWIRE_REMOTE jail env; gated on ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/pulse/native

Bundled loophole: tls-intercept platform.claude.com→127.0.0.1, ca_cert={state}/ca.crt, host_daemon yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host, jail_daemon python3 -m src.oauth_broker_jail (on-failure); gated on `claude` on PATH
Bundled loophole: unix-socket/spawned; host_daemon yolo-host-processes --socket {socket}; doctor --self-check; gated on `ps` on PATH

public_surface.name: yolo loopholes list
public_surface.detail: Lists all loopholes (include_disabled) with state label active|disabled|inactive (reason), format: '  {label:<36}  {name}  ({source}/{transport}/{lifecycle})  {extra}' where extra is 'intercepts=[hosts]' for tls-intercept-with-intercepts else 'transport=X'; description indented 6 spaces on next line. Empty case prints 'No loopholes installed.' plus the 3 source locations.

public_surface.name: yolo loopholes status
public_surface.detail: Runs each doctor_cmd (10s timeout, capture stdout-else-stderr). Prefix per line: [disabled]|[inactive]|[ok rc==0]|[no-check rc==None]|[fail], format '  [{prefix}] {name}  rc={rc}' + indented output lines. In-jail (YOLO_VERSION set): prints redirect message and returns.

public_surface.name: yolo loopholes enable <name> / disable <name>
public_surface.detail: Toggles 'enabled' in ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/loopholes/<name>/manifest.jsonc only (user dir). Missing manifest → stderr message + exit 1. Rewrite: 2-line comment header + json.dumps(indent=2) + newline (JSONC comments in body are LOST — parse via pyjson5, re-serialize as plain JSON). Prints 'enabled <name>'/'disabled <name>'.

public_surface.name: yolo broker {status,stop,restart}
public_surface.detail: broker_cmd.py consumes _broker_status/_broker_kill/_broker_spawn/_broker_is_alive from this module (broker_cmd itself is another group, but these functions are this module's exported surface)

public_surface.name: manifest.jsonc schema v1
public_surface.detail: Keys: name (req, must == dir name), description (req str), version, enabled (default true), transport ∈ {tls-intercept,unix-socket,none} (default tls-intercept), lifecycle ∈ {external,spawned} (default external), intercepts [{host}], broker_ip (default 'host-gateway'), ca_cert (supports {state} template → ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/state/<name>/; else relative to manifest dir), jail_env {str:str}, doctor_cmd [str], host_daemon {cmd:[str] with {socket} placeholder, env}, jail_daemon {cmd:[str], restart ∈ {always,on-failure,no} default on-failure}, host_bind_mounts [{host (supports {loophole_dir} then ${VAR}/$VAR expansion; unresolved var → empty), container, readonly bool default true}], host_devices [str], requires {command_on_path, file_exists (env-expanded; unresolved → empty → never matches)}. Malformed manifests raise LoopholeError; discovery skips them silently, validate_loopholes reports them.
public_surface.consumers: third-party loophole authors, yolo check, docs/loopholes.md

public_surface.name: ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/loopholes/<name>/
public_surface.detail: User loopholes dir; overrides bundled on name collision (full manifest replacement). Hidden dirs and non-dirs skipped; sorted iteration order.

public_surface.name: <wheel>/src/bundled_loopholes/<name>/
public_surface.detail: Bundled loopholes shipped in wheel (pyproject package-data '**/*'); read-only; source label 'bundled'

public_surface.name: ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/state/<name>/
public_surface.detail: Writable per-loophole state dir ({state} template target); mounted ro into jail at /var/lib/yolo-jail/loopholes/<name> when jail_daemon declared and dir exists

public_surface.name: yolo-jail.jsonc loopholes.<name>
public_surface.detail: Workspace source. If name matches a file-backed loophole: only enabled (replace), env (shallow-merge into host_daemon.env), jail_env (shallow-merge) are honored. Otherwise synthesized as inline loophole: transport=unix-socket, lifecycle=spawned, fields description/enabled/doctor_cmd/command/env/jail_socket; command must contain {socket} placeholder. Names 'cgroup-delegate' and 'journal' reserved (skipped).

public_surface.name: yolo-jail.jsonc journal
public_surface.detail: true→'user', false/missing→'off', or string off|user|full. 'user' prepends --user to journalctl args. Enables builtin journal bridge.

public_surface.name: container runtime flags emitted by runtime_args_for()
public_surface.detail: Per active file-backed loophole: --add-host {host}:{broker_ip} per intercept; -v {loophole_dir}:/etc/yolo-jail/loopholes/{name}:ro + -v {state_dir}:/var/lib/yolo-jail/loopholes/{name}:ro (only when jail_daemon declared; state only if dir exists); CA path computed from existing dir mount when relative (never file-over-dir mount — podman rejects), else -v file mount to /etc/yolo-jail/loopholes/{name}/ca.crt; -v host:container[:ro] per existing host_bind_mount (missing source → warn+skip); --device per existing host_device (skipped entirely in-jail when YOLO_VERSION set); -e K=V per jail_env; final -e NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=<os.pathsep-joined container CA paths>; final -e YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS=<JSON [{name,cmd,restart}]>. runtime=='container' (Apple Container) skips tls-intercept loopholes entirely.

public_surface.name: YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS
public_surface.detail: JSON list [{name,cmd:[str],restart}] set on container; consumed by src/jail_daemon_supervisor.py (spawned by entrypoint/runtime.py) which supervises jail-side daemons until PID1 exit

public_surface.name: NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS
public_surface.detail: os.pathsep-joined list of in-jail CA cert paths from active tls-intercept loopholes

public_surface.name: YOLO_SERVICE_<NAME>_SOCKET
public_surface.detail: Per-service jail env: name sanitized re.sub('[^A-Za-z0-9]+','_').strip('_').upper(). Value = jail_socket_path (default /run/yolo-services/<name>.sock, overridable via config jail_socket). Broker's var set specially in run_cmd (singleton returns no handle). Examples: YOLO_SERVICE_CGROUP_DELEGATE_SOCKET, YOLO_SERVICE_JOURNAL_SOCKET, YOLO_SERVICE_HOST_PROCESSES_SOCKET, YOLO_SERVICE_CLAUDE_OAUTH_BROKER_SOCKET
public_surface.consumers: yolo-ps, yolo-journalctl, yolo-cglimit, oauth_broker_jail (all jail side)

public_surface.name: YOLO_VERSION (read)
public_surface.detail: Presence = running inside a jail. Switches requirements_met to container-side bind-mount-existence check, suppresses device passthrough, redirects `loopholes status` to host

public_surface.name: XDG_RUNTIME_DIR (read)
public_surface.detail: Expanded in audio manifest's requires.file_exists and host_bind_mounts host paths at parse time on the host

public_surface.name: /tmp/yolo-host-services-<sha1(cname)[:8]>/
public_surface.detail: Per-jail host-side sockets dir (kept under /tmp for AF_UNIX 108/104-byte path limits; macOS uses resolved /private/tmp). Bind-mounted rw into jail at /run/yolo-services. Contains <service>.sock files chmod 0777 (builtins) / daemon-owned (external). rmtree'd by stop_loopholes (guarded: skip if container still running or workspace lock busy).

public_surface.name: /tmp/yolo-claude-oauth-broker.{sock,pid,lock}
public_surface.detail: Broker singleton well-known socket, PID file (int + \n), and flock file. Spawn: flock LOCK_EX on .lock, re-check alive (pid live + socket exists + ping ok), unlink stale sock, Popen yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host --socket <sock> with start_new_session, stdout/err → ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/logs/host-service-claude-oauth-broker.log, write pid, poll for socket ≤5s at 0.05s.

public_surface.name: /tmp/yolo-broker-relay-<hash8>.{pid,lock}
public_surface.detail: Per-jail relay supervision files (hash = sha1(cname)[:8], same as sockets dir). Relay spawned as `sys.executable <repo>/src/broker_relay.py --socket <sockets_dir>/claude-oauth-broker.sock --broker /tmp/yolo-claude-oauth-broker.sock --jail <cname>` by absolute path (never -m). Logs → GLOBAL_STORAGE/logs/broker-relay-<hash8>.log. Liveness: pid live + socket connectable; missing pidfile + connectable socket = alive (tmpfiles aging). Kill guarded by /proc/<pid>/cmdline (or `ps -p ... -o args=` on macOS) containing 'broker_relay.py'. Orphan sweep: _relay_reap_orphans globs /tmp/yolo-broker-relay-*.pid, spares live cname hashes and files younger than 3600s, declines if live set unknown (None).
public_surface.consumers: run_cmd (_ensure_broker_relay on run+attach), stop_loopholes, yolo prune

public_surface.name: cgroup-delegate JSON protocol
public_surface.detail: Socket <sockets_dir>/cgroup-delegate.sock (0777). Request: single JSON line ≤4096B. Ops: status → {ok,delegated,controllers,cgroup}; create_and_join {name,cpu_pct,memory,pids} → validates name ^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]{0,63}$, gets caller host-PID via SO_PEERCRED (Linux)/LOCAL_PEERPID 0x002 (macOS), builds <container_cgroup>/{init,agent}/ hierarchy (moves existing procs to init, enables +cpu +memory +pids subtree_control), creates agent/<name>, writes cpu.max ('{pct*1000*nproc} 100000'), memory.max (k/m/g suffixes, min 1MiB), pids.max (1..1000000), moves peer PID in; response {ok,cgroup,warnings?}; destroy {name} → rmdir if empty of procs (idempotent ok). Response: single JSON line + \n. Every request logged to GLOBAL_STORAGE/logs/<cname>-cgd.log. Skipped on macOS and non-cgroupv2 hosts (returns None). Container cgroup lazily resolved on first request via _resolve_container_cgroup.

public_surface.name: journal bridge framed protocol
public_surface.detail: Socket <sockets_dir>/journal.sock (0777). Request: one JSON line {args:[str]} ≤16384B header, ≤64 args, each ≤1024B. mode 'user' prepends --user. Spawns host journalctl (start_new_session), pumps stdout/stderr as frames [stream:u8][len:u32 BE][payload]; streams 1=stdout 2=stderr 3=exit (payload int32 BE). Errors: malformed request → stderr frame + exit 2; journalctl missing → exit 127; spawn OSError → exit 1. Client disconnect kills journalctl. Logged to GLOBAL_STORAGE/logs/<cname>-journal.log. NOTE stream IDs differ from loophole-protocol v1 (which is 0=stdout,1=stderr,2=exit).

public_surface.name: broker ping (loophole frame protocol v1 subset)
public_surface.detail: _broker_ping: send 4-byte BE length + '{"action":"ping"}'; read 5-byte (sid:u8,len:u32 BE) frames; sid 0 payload JSON with pong:true → alive; sid 2 (exit) first → dead. 2s timeout. Full protocol spec in docs/loophole-protocol.md (v1: streams 0/1/2, exit payload int32 BE).

public_surface.name: ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/logs/
public_surface.detail: All daemon logs: host-service-<name>.log (external, append binary), <cname>-cgd.log, <cname>-journal.log, broker-relay-<hash8>.log, host-service-claude-oauth-broker.log

public_surface.name: ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/locks/<cname>.lock
public_surface.detail: Per-workspace flock taken non-blocking by stop_loopholes to detect a mid-flight relaunch (busy → skip relay reap + rmtree)

public_surface.name: loopholes enable/disable failure
public_surface.detail: typer.Exit(1) when no user-installed manifest at the path

public_surface.name: SIGTERM/SIGKILL escalation
public_surface.detail: External services: SIGTERM → 5s grace → SIGKILL → 2s reap. Relay: SIGTERM → 3s poll (with waitpid WNOHANG zombie reaping of own children) → SIGKILL → 1s reap. Broker kill: same 3s pattern, plus pgrep -f fallback ('yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host' / 'broker_relay.py --socket <path>') for stale/missing pidfiles.

public_surface.name: jail container paths
public_surface.detail: /etc/yolo-jail/loopholes/<name>/ (ro loophole dir), /var/lib/yolo-jail/loopholes/<name>/ (ro state), /run/yolo-services/ (rw sockets dir, JAIL_HOST_SERVICES_DIR), audio: /run/pulse/native, /run/pipewire/pipewire-0, /etc/asound.conf, /dev/snd
public_surface.consumers: jail-side clients + daemons; parity tests must preserve exactly

internal_deps: src/cli/paths.py (USER_CONFIG_PATH, GLOBAL_STORAGE, JAIL_HOST_SERVICES_DIR, CGD_SOCKET_NAME, JOURNAL_SOCKET_NAME, BUILTIN_*_LOOPHOLE_NAME, IS_LINUX, IS_MACOS)

consumed by: src/cli/run_cmd.py, src/cli/broker_cmd.py, src/cli/check_cmd.py, src/cli/prune_cmd.py, src/cli/config.py (_known_loopholes), src/cli/__init__.py; jail side: src/jail_daemon_supervisor.py + src/entrypoint/runtime.py (YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS), src/entrypoint/scripts.py (yolo-journalctl client), src/yolo_ps.py, src/oauth_broker_jail.py

third_party_deps.go_equivalent: github.com/tailscale/hujson or muhammadmuzzammil1998/jsonc — MUST also accept JSON5-isms if any manifests use them (current bundled ones use only // comments)
fatih/color or plain ANSI; parity tests should strip color

side_effects: Spawns detached host processes (broker singleton, per-jail relay, external services) with start_new_session; logs appended under ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/logs/
Creates/removes /tmp/yolo-host-services-<hash>/ dirs and sockets; chmod 0777 on builtin sockets
Rewrites user manifest.jsonc on enable/disable (drops comments)
Writes into /sys/fs/cgroup subtree (cgroup delegate: mkdir, cgroup.procs, cpu.max, memory.max, pids.max, subtree_control, rmdir)
Spawns host journalctl per journal request; kills it on client disconnect
Runs doctor_cmd subprocesses (10s timeout) and nvidia-smi/rocminfo/pgrep/ps probes
kill()/waitpid on relay/broker PIDs incl. SIGKILL escalation; rmtree of sockets dirs
python_specifics: In-process daemon threads: builtin cgroup + journal services run as threads inside the `yolo run` process with threading.Event shutdown and a dummy-connect accept wake (_wake_and_close_listener); in Go these become goroutines + listener.Close(), but the parity point is socket path/perm/protocol, not threading
fcntl.flock serialization (broker spawn lock, relay lock, per-workspace stop lock with LOCK_NB) — Go: golang.org/x/sys/unix.Flock; semantics must match (advisory, per-open-file)

os.waitpid(pid, WNOHANG) zombie reaping of direct children (_reap_if_child) — relay is a direct child of the yolo process; Go can't waitpid arbitrary PIDs it didn't spawn via os/exec without exec.Cmd handle; a Go port that spawns the relay itself can use cmd.Process/Wait, but killing relays spawned by an OLDER Python yolo needs raw unix.Wait4 (will fail with ECHILD → same fallback path)
SO_PEERCRED via getsockopt struct '3i' unpack (Linux) and LOCAL_PEERPID=0x002 (macOS) — Go: unix.GetsockoptUcred / unix.GetsockoptInt(0, 0x002)
subprocess.Popen(start_new_session=True, close_fds=True) detach pattern — Go: SysProcAttr{Setsid: true}

Relay launched as `sys.executable /path/src/broker_relay.py` — a Go port of loopholes_runtime that keeps the Python relay must locate a python3 + the script; conversely porting broker_relay first removes this (see seams)

pyjson5 leniency + set_enabled round-trip via json.dumps(indent=2): comments destroyed, key order = Python dict insertion order of parsed manifest — byte-exact parity requires matching Go map→ordered handling
_expand_env regex ${VAR}|$VAR with unresolved→EMPTY (deliberately unlike shell/os.ExpandEnv which Go's os.Expand can replicate with a mapper returning "")
os.pathsep join for NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS (':' on POSIX)
hashlib.sha1(cname)[:8] naming shared by sockets dir, relay pid/lock, logs — any port must keep byte-identical hashing
socket.settimeout(1.0) accept tick as shutdown fallback; srv.close() double-close tolerance
Reading /proc/<pid>/cmdline (NUL-separated) with `ps -p PID -o args=` macOS fallback

Path(__file__).parent-relative discovery of bundled_loopholes and broker_relay.py — Go binary must embed bundled manifests (go:embed) or ship them at a known path; embedding changes runtime_args_for's `-v {m.path}:...` dir mount, which mounts the real bundled dir into the jail — Go needs an on-disk materialization

seams.name: runtime_args_for as pure function
seams.contract: stdin: set of manifest dirs + loopholes config + runtime name + env (XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, YOLO_VERSION); stdout: ordered argv fragment ([--add-host, -v, --device, -e ...]) exactly as Python emits, including NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS and YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS JSON
seams.swap_mechanism: Extract as `yolo-loophole-args` subprocess (Go binary) called from Python run_cmd behind env flag YOLO_GO_LOOPHOLES=1; Python keeps discovery or Go does both
seams.parity_test: Golden tests: run Python runtime_args_for and Go binary over the 56 fixture manifests in tests/test_loopholes.py; diff argv lists byte-for-byte (JSON payload compared parsed AND serialized)

seams.name: broker relay process (src/broker_relay.py)
seams.contract: argv: --socket <path> --broker <path> --jail <name>; binds AF_UNIX listener, per-connection dials broker, injects jail_id into length-prefixed JSON request, byte-proxies frames; detached, logs to stderr→file
seams.swap_mechanism: _relay_ensure launches by path today; change spawn argv to a Go binary path (config/env YOLO_BROKER_RELAY_BIN) — cmdline identity guard `broker_relay.py` in _relay_pid_cmdline_matches/_relay_pgrep must be updated in lockstep (match on binary name)
seams.parity_test: tests/test_broker_relay.py (8 tests) against either implementation: request stamping, byte-proxy fidelity, EOF handling, socket bind

seams.name: host-side loophole daemons (yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host, yolo-host-processes)
seams.contract: argv contains --socket {socket}; must bind the socket within 5s; speak frame protocol v1 (docs/loophole-protocol.md: 4B BE len JSON request; frames sid∈{0,1,2}, exit=int32 BE); --self-check exit 0 = healthy; SIGTERM clean exit within 5s
seams.swap_mechanism: These are console-script names in manifests — replace the entry point with a Go binary of the same name on PATH (or edit user manifest cmd); zero changes to the loader
seams.parity_test: Ping round-trip via _broker_ping; yolo loopholes status rc; protocol conformance harness sending each request shape and diffing frames

seams.name: builtin cgroup-delegate + journal daemons
seams.contract: Socket names cgroup-delegate.sock / journal.sock in sockets dir, chmod 0777; exact JSON-line (cgd) and framed 1/2/3 (journal) protocols above; skip conditions (macOS, no cgroupv2, no journalctl) identical
seams.swap_mechanism: Currently threads in yolo run — carve out as spawned subprocesses first (matching _start_host_service_external shape), then swap binary; or swap whole `yolo run` at once
seams.parity_test: In-jail clients yolo-cglimit / yolo-journalctl driven end-to-end in a nested jail; unit tests test_cli_unit.py cgroup/journal cases

seams.name: jail-side supervisor boundary (YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS env)
seams.contract: JSON list [{name, cmd:[str], restart:always|on-failure|no}] — producer (host runtime_args_for) and consumer (jail src/jail_daemon_supervisor.py) can be ported independently
seams.swap_mechanism: Env var is the process boundary; either side swaps freely

seams.name: whole `yolo loopholes` subcommand
seams.contract: stdout text formats documented in public_surface; exit codes; manifest rewrite format of enable/disable
seams.swap_mechanism: Shell out from Typer to Go `yolo-loopholes` binary, or route the subcommand in a Go top-level CLI while `yolo run` stays Python
seams.parity_test: Snapshot tests of list/status/enable/disable output over fixture trees (tests/test_cli_commands.py already snapshot-ish)

seams.name: broker/relay supervision files
seams.swap_mechanism: Any language can ensure/kill against these paths; Go `yolo broker` subcommand can ship before Go `yolo run`
seams.parity_test: Spawn with Python, status/stop with Go and vice versa; test_state_separation.py patterns

existing: tests/test_loopholes.py — 56 tests: manifest parse/validate, discovery merge (bundled<user<workspace), requires gating incl. env collapse, in-jail activation, runtime_args_for goldens (intercepts, CA merge, dir-vs-file mounts, bind mounts, devices, Apple Container skip, in-jail device skip), bundled audio manifest, set_enabled roundtrip, doctor checks

tests/test_cli_unit.py — 459 tests incl. cgroup delegate hierarchy/limits, journal daemon skip, relay ensure/kill/cmdline-guard/orphan reap, stop_loopholes guards (live container, relaunch lock, rmtree ordering), start_loopholes reserved names + AC skip
tests/test_broker_relay.py — 8 tests on the relay process
tests/test_state_separation.py, tests/test_macos_paths.py — /tmp vs state dirs, /private/tmp resolution
gaps: No end-to-end test of broker singleton spawn/flock race (only unit-level)
Journal framed protocol has no protocol-level golden test (client is generated shell/python in entrypoint)
_broker_ping frame parsing untested against a real broker in unit suite
enable/disable comment-loss rewrite has roundtrip test but no byte-exact golden
test_coverage.assessment: Strong for the registry (loopholes.py is near-fully covered and the goldens double as Go parity fixtures); moderate for lifecycle (supervision logic covered via mocks, real process/socket races only lightly); protocol conformance tests should be added before porting daemons

porting_hazards: set_enabled rewrites JSONC as plain JSON (comments lost, dict order preserved) — Go must reproduce this exact degradation or improve it consistently across A/B (parity tests will diff manifests)

Bundled manifests located via Path(__file__).parent; runtime_args_for bind-mounts the actual bundled dir (m.path) into the jail — a Go binary must ship bundled_loopholes on disk (not just embedded) or materialize them, and the mounted path appears in podman argv (parity-visible)

Relay spawn uses sys.executable + broker_relay.py path AND identity-guards via cmdline substring 'broker_relay.py' (pgrep -f and /proc cmdline) — porting the relay to Go breaks the guard/pgrep patterns unless coordinated; mixed Python-yolo/Go-relay (or reverse) deployments will fail to reap each other's relays
Journal stream IDs (1=stdout,2=stderr,3=exit) intentionally differ from loophole-protocol v1 (0/1/2) — easy to conflate when porting both
_expand_env: unresolved ${VAR} → empty string, NOT shell passthrough; getting this wrong silently changes audio activation and bind-mount sources

Ordering matters: runtime_args_for emits flags in manifest-sorted, per-loophole, fixed-internal order; NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS and YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS appended last — argv diffs are the parity test, so preserve order exactly (incl. dict iteration order for jail_env: Python dicts are insertion-ordered from JSONC; Go maps are NOT — need ordered map)

YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS uses json.dumps default separators (', ', ': ') — Go encoding/json produces different whitespace; consumer parses JSON so semantic parity suffices, but byte-golden tests must compare parsed

Liveness polarity rules encode incident history: missing relay pidfile+connectable socket = ALIVE; _relay_reap_orphans(None) = decline; EPERM on kill(pid,0) = alive; zombie child = alive to kill(0) hence _reap_if_child — replicate exactly or re-introduce the 2026 incidents

stop_loopholes guard stack (non-blocking workspace flock → still-running container check → relay kill BEFORE rmtree) is order-sensitive and race-hardened; the lock file handle must stay open for the duration
macOS specifics: /tmp→/private/tmp resolution in _host_service_sockets_dir, LOCAL_PEERPID, ps fallback, no cgroup delegate, ENOTCONN-on-shutdown motivating the dummy-connect wake
cgroup delegate writes to /sys/fs/cgroup as the invoking user (rootless podman delegation) — needs same UID semantics; peer PID from SO_PEERCRED is host-namespace by construction
chmod 0777 on builtin sockets is load-bearing (container mapped UID); frame headers are struct '>BI' / '>i' big-endian

broker singleton returns NO LoopholeDaemon handle by design (must not be reaped per-jail) and the relay is also not a handle (must survive yolo death) — a naive Go lifecycle that treats everything as handles regresses both incidents

Timing knobs (5.0 spawn, 0.05 poll, 3.0 kill, 1.0 reap, 5.0/2.0 external graces, 3600 orphan grace, 10.0 doctor timeout, 1.0 accept tick, 2.0 ping timeout) are documented as behavior-frozen — keep values
notes: Split cleanly in two: (1) src/loopholes.py registry is pure/deterministic and the easiest first Go target — its 56 tests are ready-made parity fixtures via a `manifests+config in → argv out` golden harness; (2) loopholes_runtime.py is entangled with run_cmd's lifecycle (start/stop called around container run, relay ensured on attach paths too) and should port together with run_cmd or be carved into spawned subprocesses first. The broker singleton/relay filesystem protocol (/tmp pid+lock+sock files, sha1[:8] keying) is the interop contract that lets Go and Python yolo invocations coexist during incremental rollout — freeze it. docs/loophole-protocol.md v1 (streams 0/1/2) governs external daemons; the journal bridge uses a DIFFERENT private framing (1/2/3). Related but out-of-group: src/broker_relay.py, src/host_service.py, src/host_processes.py, src/oauth_broker_jail.py, src/jail_daemon_supervisor.py, src/yolo_ps.py, src/cli/broker_cmd.py, src/cli/run_cmd.py wiring (lines ~2312-3030), src/cli/check_cmd.py doctor integration.
